changes.to:
Goal
Improve hstry for decision-oriented history analysis so agents can produce high-signal summaries with minimal token usage and fewer manual grep/show loops.

Context / Problem
Current analysis workflow requires broad semantic search + large  dumps. Results are noisy (duplicate/boilerplate hits), expensive in tokens, and weakly structured for extracting architectural decisions and open questions.

Scope
This epic delivers:
1) decision-oriented derived metadata,
2) a first-class analysis command with evidence pointers,
3) budget-aware focused retrieval modes,
4) result dedup/filter improvements.

Non-goals
- Replacing oqto-log as runtime authority.
- Changing adapter ingestion semantics unrelated to analysis.

Positioning
- oqto-log remains authoritative runtime timeline store for Oqto sessions.
- hstry remains cross-source analysis/search/projection layer.
- New features optimize hstry’s analysis role, not live-session authority.

Acceptance Criteria
- Agent can run one command flow and get: decisions, open questions, and exact evidence pointers (conv id + message idx/quote) within a fixed token budget.
- Search results are materially less noisy via dedup/clustering and structured filters.
- Focused retrieval can cap output by token/char budget and prioritize decision-rich segments.
- Docs include recommended workflows for agentic analysis.

Deliverables
- Linked child issues: decision snapshots, analyze command, focused show/export, search dedup/filters.
- CLI docs and examples.
- Tests for new analyzers/filters/output modes.

session_name: oqto_refactor: Oqto-Log Migration Delay During Deploy [bent-melt-then]

changes.to:
Feature: Decision snapshot metadata model + incremental updater

Problem
Decision extraction currently re-processes full transcripts repeatedly. We need compact derived state per conversation for fast, low-token analysis.

Design
Add derived snapshot structure stored per conversation (table or metadata blob; choose via schema review):
- decisions[]: {id, statement, status(active/superseded), tags[], confidence}
- open_questions[]: {id, question, owner?, status}
- contracts[]: {area, rule, source}
- evidence[] refs: conversation_id, message_idx, short_quote
- updated_at, source_version

Update strategy
- Incremental updater processes only new messages since last snapshot watermark.
- Idempotent recompute fallback for corruption/version bumps.
- Schema versioning for snapshot format.

CLI/API
- Internal service methods to get snapshot by conversation and refresh on demand.
- Optional  hook.

Acceptance Criteria
- Snapshot generation is deterministic for same input.
- Incremental updates avoid full transcript scan in normal path.
- Snapshot includes evidence refs usable by downstream commands.
- Unit tests cover update, replay, and version migration behavior.

Implementation Notes for next agent
- Start with minimal MVP fields (decisions/open_questions/evidence), expand later.
- Keep extraction heuristic/simple parser first; avoid heavy model calls in core path.
- Document confidence limitations clearly.

Data source order

Acceptance Criteria

- Works in JSON mode for agent pipelines.

- Add stable output contract early and freeze it for downstream agents/tools.

changes.to:
Feature: token-budgeted focused show/export modes

Problem
 often emits huge outputs, causing token waste and broken-pipe workflows.

Additions
- 
-  (or char proxy if tokenizer unavailable)
- 
-  mode: emit only refs + short quotes

Selection strategy
- Rank segments by relevance to requested focus.
- Preserve chronological coherence where possible.
- Hard-stop at budget with explicit truncation metadata.

- Output size is bounded predictably.
- Truncation is explicit and machine-readable in JSON mode.

session_name: oqto_refactor: Oqto-Log Migration Delay During Deploy [bent-melt-then]

changes.to:
Feature: search dedup/clustering + structured filter fields

Problem
Search returns many near-duplicate/boilerplate hits, reducing signal-to-noise.

Scope
1) Dedup/clustering
- Group near-identical hits by conversation/title/snippet similarity.
- Return representative hit + count of collapsed items.

2) Structured filters
- Filter by source, repo/workspace, role, date window, tags (if available), has_decisions.
- Query examples:
  - No results found.
  - 

3) Output improvements
-  to limit payload (id,title,score,snippet)
- clearer ranking rationale (optional debug mode)

Acceptance Criteria
- Duplicate-heavy queries return materially fewer redundant rows.
- Filters are combinable and documented.
- JSON output includes cluster metadata when dedup enabled.

Implementation Notes
- Keep dedup optional via flag first (), then consider default-on after validation.
- Add benchmark fixture to compare precision/noise before vs after.

Feature: token-budgeted focused show/export modes

Problem
`hstry show` often emits huge outputs, causing token waste and broken-pipe workflows.

Additions
- `hstry show <id> --focus decisions|timeline|tools|all`
- `hstry show <id> --budget-tokens <n>` (or char proxy if tokenizer unavailable)
- `hstry export ... --focus ... --budget-*`
- `--evidence-only` mode: emit only refs + short quotes

Selection strategy
- Rank segments by relevance to requested focus.
- Preserve chronological coherence where possible.
- Hard-stop at budget with explicit truncation metadata.

Acceptance Criteria
- Output size is bounded predictably.
- Truncation is explicit and machine-readable in JSON mode.
- Significantly lower token footprint for common analysis tasks.

Implementation Notes
- Start with char-budget fallback and pluggable tokenizer interface.
- Keep backward compatibility for default `show` behavior.

changes.from: claude-code adapter detect() too greedy: matches JSONLs inside pi/codex trees, allows duplicate sources
changes.to: Source-registration invariant: only canonical adapter roots may become sources (eliminates spurious-source class)

changes.from:
## Symptoms

`hstry source list` shows **45 `claude-code` sources** when there should be one:

- 1 canonical → `/home/wismut/.claude/projects` ✓
- 1 **duplicate** → `claude-code-7acee973` also points at `/home/wismut/.claude/projects` (bug: source-add accepted a path that already had a source registered)
- 39 spurious → individual `reports.jsonl` files under `~/.pi/agent/sessions/*/workers/*/reports/`
- 4 spurious → individual rollout `.jsonl` files under `~/.codex/sessions/2026/02/…`

`hstry sync` therefore does 45 redundant passes, most of them logging `No existing conversations; resetting sync cursor` followed by `No new conversations`.

## Root cause

The claude-code adapter's `detect()` matches the JSONL line-shape regardless of *where the file lives*. When the source-add path walked the pi-agent session trees and the codex session tree, every individual JSONL inside them was identified as claude-code and auto-registered as its own source — instead of being claimed by the pi or codex adapters that own those trees.

Reference: `adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts` `detect()` method; `crates/hstry-runtime/src/runner.rs` AdapterRunner.

## Two related bugs

1. **Greedy detection across harness territory.** claude-code's `detect()` should refuse paths under directories owned by another harness (`~/.pi/`, `~/.codex/`, `~/.local/share/opencode/`). The pi and codex adapters already root their canonical sources at those trees — anything inside them belongs to that harness, not claude-code, even if the JSONL line-shape happens to look claude-code-flavored (which it does for pi workers, since pi orchestrates Claude Code).

2. **Source-add accepts duplicate paths.** Adding a source whose path is already registered (canonical or otherwise) should be a no-op or an error, not a new source row with a different id. The duplicate `claude-code-7acee973` proves this guard is missing.

## Recommended fix (option 3 from discussion)

**Don't auto-register sources per individual file. Only register directory roots.** Individual JSONLs become discoveries *under* an existing source; they never get their own source row. This eliminates both bugs at the data-model level:

- A reports.jsonl inside `~/.pi/...` cannot become a source — only `~/.pi/agent/sessions` (the pi root) can be a source.
- A rollout-*.jsonl inside `~/.codex/...` cannot become a source — only `~/.codex/sessions` (the codex root) can be a source.
- Duplicates of `/home/wismut/.claude/projects` cannot exist because that root already has a source.

This is the most invasive of the three options considered but eliminates the class of bug instead of patching specific symptoms.

## Alternatives (lower effort, less robust)

- **(1) Tighten claude-code `detect()`** to reject paths under `~/.pi/`, `~/.codex/`, `~/.local/share/opencode/`. Quick patch but easily out-of-date when a new harness is added.
- **(2) Adapter priority in detection.** Score pi/codex/opencode adapters higher than claude-code for paths inside their own tree. Cleaner than (1), but still per-file source registration remains possible if all adapters happen to reject a path then someone adds it manually.

## Cleanup needed

Separate from the fix: 44 spurious sources need to be removed (39 pi + 4 codex + 1 dup-canonical). Tracked here as part of this ticket's resolution — once the fix lands the cleanup is one-shot.

## Validation

After fix:

- `hstry source list | wc -l` returns ~5 (claude-code, claude-web, codex-main, opencode, pi/import-pi).
- `hstry sync` output has one line per source, no `resetting sync cursor` spam.
- Attempting `hstry source add /home/wismut/.claude/projects` a second time returns an error or no-op, not a new source id.
- Attempting `hstry source add /home/wismut/.pi/agent/sessions/.../reports.jsonl` returns an error (file is inside another harness's territory and not a directory root anyway).

## Reference files

- `adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts` — `detect()` impl
- `adapters/pi/adapter.ts` — owns `~/.pi/agent/sessions`
- `adapters/codex/adapter.ts` — owns `~/.codex/sessions`
- `crates/hstry-runtime/src/runner.rs` — AdapterRunner, detection dispatch
- Likely a source-add path in the hstry CLI (cmd module) that needs the duplicate-path guard
changes.to:
## Hard invariant (must hold at all times)

**A registered source's `path` MUST be a canonical adapter root.** Every other path — a file inside an adapter root, a subdirectory of another source's root, a duplicate of an existing source's path — MUST be refused at the point of registration. There is no second-chance reconciliation; the only fix is preventing bad sources from being created in the first place.

We just lived through what happens when this invariant is violated: 45 spurious `claude-code-*` sources (39 inside `~/.pi/`, 4 inside `~/.codex/`, 1 duplicate of canonical), plus 1 redundant `import-pi` sub-source. Cleanup required a reseed of `pi` (purge + re-import of ~2200 conversations) and a reseed of `claude-code`. This must never recur.

## What "must never happen" means concretely

Every code path that can create a source row MUST refuse:

1. **Duplicate path.** Adding a path equal to an existing source's path (any adapter). Return error or no-op; never a new source id.
2. **Sub-path of an existing source.** Adding a directory that is a strict descendant of an existing source's path. The parent already covers it.
3. **Super-path of an existing source.** Adding a directory that is a strict ancestor of an existing source's path. Either reject, or atomically replace the descendant with the ancestor (one or the other — never both).
4. **Individual file paths.** A source's path must be a directory. Files inside an adapter's canonical tree are discoveries, not sources.
5. **Cross-harness territory.** A non-`pi` adapter cannot register a path under another harness's canonical root (e.g. claude-code cannot register inside `~/.pi/`, `~/.codex/`, `~/.local/share/opencode/`). Each harness owns its tree.

Code paths to audit: `hstry source add`, `hstry scan`, `hstry quickstart`, `hstry import` (any auto-registration it does), the gRPC/service endpoints if any.

## Detection-side rule (defense in depth)

Adapter `detect()` must score 0 (not just lower) for paths outside its canonical territory. claude-code currently scores 0.9 on a file at `~/.pi/agent/sessions/.../*.jsonl`; pi scores 0.95 there. Both > 0 means a competitive tie-breaker mistake can still register the wrong adapter. The right rule:

- Each adapter declares a canonical-root predicate (e.g. claude-code = `~/.claude/projects`, pi = `~/.pi/agent/sessions`, codex = `~/.codex/sessions`, opencode = `~/.local/share/opencode`).
- `detect()` returns > 0 only when the path is *under that root*.
- If multiple adapters declare overlapping roots (e.g. pi orchestrates Claude Code internally and emits Claude-Code-format JSONLs inside its own tree), the more-specific root wins by construction.

This makes the source-registration invariant trivially enforceable: a path either falls inside exactly one adapter's territory or outside all of them.

## Concrete validation tests

After fix, these MUST all hold (treat as test cases):

- `hstry source add /home/wismut/.claude/projects` succeeds once, fails (or no-ops) on the second invocation.
- `hstry source add ~/.pi/agent/sessions/--home-wismut-byteowlz-octo--` returns error: "subpath of existing source `pi` (~/.pi/agent/sessions); use the parent root".
- `hstry source add ~/.pi/agent/sessions/.../some-session.jsonl` returns error: "source path must be a directory, not a file".
- `hstry scan ~/.pi/` discovers conversations *under the existing pi source*, never proposes a new source row for sub-paths.
- `hstry quickstart` cannot, under any path the user might point it at, end up registering more than one source per (adapter, canonical-root) pair.

## Symptoms we just cleaned up (for context)

- 1 duplicate canonical claude-code (`claude-code-7acee973`) pointing at the same `~/.claude/projects`.
- 39 spurious `claude-code-*` sources pointing at individual `reports.jsonl` files inside `~/.pi/agent/sessions/*/workers/*/reports/`.
- 4 spurious `claude-code-*` sources pointing at individual rollout `.jsonl` files inside `~/.codex/sessions/2026/02/…`.
- 1 redundant `import-pi` source rooted at `~/.pi/agent/sessions/--home-wismut-byteowlz-oqto_refactor--` (subpath of `pi`), holding 302 duplicate conversations.

All gone now, but only because of a manual reseed-and-cleanup pass. The cleanup is one bad `hstry scan` away from undoing itself until this lands.

## Reference files

- `adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts` — `detect()` impl, must declare canonical root
- `adapters/pi/adapter.ts` — owns `~/.pi/agent/sessions`
- `adapters/codex/adapter.ts` — owns `~/.codex/sessions`
- `adapters/opencode/adapter.ts` — owns `~/.local/share/opencode`
- `crates/hstry-runtime/src/runner.rs` — AdapterRunner / dispatch
- hstry CLI source-add path — needs invariant enforcement at registration time
- hstry CLI scan / quickstart / import paths — all must refuse sub-paths / files / duplicates

## Out of scope

- Reattribution of historical conversations that were imported under the wrong source (none currently in this DB after cleanup — but if some remain, separate ticket).

note: Source-registration chokepoint landed: hstry_core::source_registry::validate_new_source enforces all 5 invariant rules (duplicate / sub-path / super-path / file-paths / cross-harness territory). Wired through cmd_source Add, cmd_import, cmd_quickstart, ensure_config_sources (CLI + service.rs), and service::detect_and_upsert. Defense in depth: claude-code, pi, codex, opencode, hermes adapters now return null in detect() for paths outside their canonical roots. 9 unit tests + live verification of all 5 trx-gzfh validation cases passed.

Fixed in adapters/claude-code/adapter.ts: stopped gating message inclusion on extractContent() returning non-empty content. Now keeps messages when parts are non-empty (tool_call, tool_result, thinking) and synthesizes a searchable content string for pure tool_use turns via summarizeToolCalls(). Verified: claude-code reseed went from 5036 to 10999 messages; peek bundles now show full tool_call/files_touched/tools data.

Shipped hstry list --peek and hstry peek <id> with PeekBundle in hstry-core::peek. Defaults: 240/240/400 chars for first_user/last_user/last_assistant, 6 bash commands @ 80 chars each, 30 files max. Adapter-agnostic: works on pi, claude-code (post-mfj4), and any adapter emitting tool_call parts. 7 unit tests cover Edit/Write/Bash mix, dedup, truncation, URL/dotfile rejection in path scanner. Verified end-to-end on real sessions: 5 sessions = ~10KB vs ~3.5MB for show.
Codex adapter v1.1.0 reads thread title/model/archived from CODEX_HOME/state_5.sqlite (readonly, graceful fallback to derived titles when DB missing/locked). Tested with fixture.
Redeployed via just update-adapters; hermes detect+parse verified working against ~/.hermes/sessions.

changes.to: Adapter implemented (v0.1.0): parses local_*.json metadata (title/archived/initialMessage) with embedded-transcript and Claude Code-style JSONL fallback; macOS/Linux/Windows roots. Field names are parsed defensively. BLOCKED ON: verification against a real Cowork session dir from a Mac (find ~/Library/Application\ Support/Claude/local-agent-mode-sessions -maxdepth 4) before closing.

note: Verified against real Cowork session from macOS desktop app: metadata fields (sessionId/cliSessionId/title/isArchived/createdAt/lastActivityAt/model/userSelectedFolders) and Claude Code-style transcript under local_<uuid>/.claude/projects/<encoded>/<cliSessionId>.jsonl. audit.jsonl used only as fallback. Adapter v0.2.0 parses title, model, workspace, 25 messages incl. tool_call/tool_result parts.

ParsedConversation/ParsedMessage/ParsedToolCall moved to hstry_core::parsed (runtime re-exports), batch write extracted to hstry_core::ingest::ingest_batch, CLI sync refactored onto it. POST /ingest added to hstry-api with bearer auth (--token/HSTRY_API_TOKEN), 64MB body limit, source auto-create. E2E tested: 401 on bad token, ingest+dedupe on repost, FTS search hit. All crate tests pass.

extension/ MV3, vanilla ES modules: background service worker with chrome.alarms polling, options page, chatgpt provider (session token via /api/auth/session, accounts/check for Teams workspaces with chatgpt-account-id header, incremental list by update_time, active-branch linearization of mapping tree).

claude provider: org discovery via /api/organizations, cookie-auth chat_conversations list+detail with tree=True rendering, content blocks mapped to canonical parts (text/thinking/tool_use/tool_result), per-org sources when multi-org.

changes.from: Reproducing  after converting a claude-code session crashes Pi FooterComponent with . Diagnose the converted JSONL schema, add a regression fixture/test that loads or validates the generated Pi session fields required by the footer, fix conversion, deploy adapters if touched, and prove the converted session starts without the footer crash.
changes.to: Reproducing hstry resume of a Claude Code session converted to Pi crashes Pi FooterComponent because a generated assistant message has invalid or missing usage fields. Diagnose the converted JSONL schema, add a regression fixture/test validating fields required by Pi footer rendering, fix conversion, deploy adapters if touched, and prove the converted session starts without the footer crash.

session_name: hstry: Hstry Sync Database Lock Error [lost-nuke-wasp]
note: Pi export now emits a complete zero-valued usage and cost structure for every assistant message, including histories without token metadata. Verified with an export fixture missing tokens/cost, validated usage.input and usage.cost.total, bundled adapter successfully, and deployed adapters via just update-adapters.
